zF_patch_unwrap_mock_aware.<locals>._mock_aware_unwrap.<locals>.<lambda>z
Got %r when unwrapping %r.  This is usually caused by a violation of Python's object protocol; see e.g. https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/5080)

_patch_unwrap_mock_aware
z5DoctestModule.collect.<locals>.MockAwareDocTestFinderz
A hackish doctest finder that overrides stdlib internals to fix a stdlib bug.
            https://github.com/pytest-dev/pytest/issues/3456
            https://bugs.python.org/issue25532
            c
Doctest code does not take into account `@property`, this
                is a hackish way to fix it.
                https://bugs.python.org/issue17446

z2_init_checker_class.<locals>.LiteralsOutputCheckerz

              (?P<mantissa>
                (?P<integer1> [+-]?\d*)\.(?P<fraction>\d+)
                |
                (?P<integer2> [+-]?\d+)\.
              )
              (?:
                [Ee]
                (?P<exponent1> [+-]?\d+)
              )?
              |
              (?P<integer3> [+-]?\d+)

                [Ee]
                (?P<exponent2> [+-]?\d+)

zM_init_checker_class.<locals>.LiteralsOutputChecker._remove_unwanted_precisionN)
compile
UNICODEr
VERBOSEr
LiteralsOutputChecker?
_init_checker_class;
Return a doctest.OutputChecker subclass that supports some
    additional options:
    * ALLOW_UNICODE and ALLOW_BYTES options to ignore u'' and b''
      prefixes (respectively) in string literals. Useful when the same
      doctest should run in Python 2 and Python 3.
    * NUMBER to ignore floating-point differences smaller than the
      precision of the literal number in the doctest.
    An inner class is used to avoid importing "doctest" at the module
    level.
    N)
z+Register and return the ALLOW_UNICODE flag.r
register_optionflagrq
z)Register and return the ALLOW_BYTES flag.r
z$Register and return the NUMBER flag.r
keyr#
Return the actual `doctest` module flag value.
    We want to do it as late as possible to avoid importing `doctest` and all
    its dependencies when parsing options, as it adds overhead and breaks tests.
    r
DOCTEST_REPORT_CHOICE_UDIFFZ
REPORT_UDIFF
DOCTEST_REPORT_CHOICE_CDIFFZ
REPORT_CDIFF
DOCTEST_REPORT_CHOICE_NDIFFZ
REPORT_NDIFF
(DOCTEST_REPORT_CHOICE_ONLY_FIRST_FAILUREZ
REPORT_ONLY_FIRST_FAILURE
DOCTEST_REPORT_CHOICE_NONE)
scopec
zaFixture that returns a :py:class:`dict` that will be injected into the
    namespace of doctests.)
